title: Sarga 76
summary: Rama's divine birth, childhood, protection of Sage Vishwamitra's rituals from demons, breaking of Shiva's bow, and marriage to Sita. Addresses youth, childhood, education, overcoming early obstacles, and marriage.

sections.summary: Themes: action and duty, devotion, mind and meditation. The passage moves from “Having heard those words of Rama, whose deeds are unblemished, the ascetic, with his head still bowed, then spoke the following words.” through “The very pleased gods then said to Rama, the one of true valor, "O gentle and great-minded one, this task for the gods has been well…” through “Rama urged the gods to revive the child, wishing them well, and reminded them that he had promised the brahmin to bring his son back to life,…”

Themes: self-knowledge, devotion, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “Having heard Rama's words, the best among the gods, pleased and filled with affection, replied to Rama.” through “Having thus agreed to the words of the gods, Rama, the delight of the Raghus, ascended that Pushpaka aerial chariot, which was adorned with gold.” through “Having saluted the great-souled Agastya, who shone as if with brilliance, and having received excellent hospitality, the king of men, Rama, then…”

Themes: action and duty, self-knowledge, devotion. The passage moves from “The greatly effulgent and great ascetic Agastya said to him, "Welcome to you, O best among men, O scion of Raghu, fortunately you have arrived."” through “It is said that giving something again, once it has been given, yields a very great reward, and indeed, you are capable of sustaining even Indra…” through “Rama further requested the sage to explain the reason for accepting such a gift, especially when given by a Brahmana, to which the great sage…”

Themes: devotion, mind and meditation, virtue and right conduct. The passage moves from “The great sage began by telling Rama that in the ancient Krita Yuga, when the world was pure, all people were without an earthly king, though…” through “Brahma then endowed him (Kṣupa) with the shares of splendor from the protectors of the worlds, and then gave King Kṣupa as the ruler of those…” through “Having accepted that excellent ornament, Rama then began to inquire about the origin of that radiant object.”

Themes: self-knowledge. The passage moves from “Rama asked, "How was this extremely wonderful, divine, and excellent form obtained by your revered self, or from where, or by whom was it taken…” through “When Rama, the descendant of Kakutstha, spoke thus, the sage then replied, "Listen, O Rama, to what happened formerly in the Treta Yuga."” through “O mighty-armed one, I saw a wonder in this beautiful forest region, which was solely concerning the power of charity. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे…”

verses.sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा रामस्याक्लिष्टकर्मणः । अवाक्छिरास्तथाभूत्वा वाक्यमेतदुवाच ह ।। 7.76.1 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard those words of Rama, whose deeds are unblemished, the ascetic, with his head still bowed, then spoke the following words.

verses.sanskrit: शूद्रयोन्यां प्रसूतो ऽस्मि शम्बूको नाम नामतः । देवत्वं पार्थये राम सशरीरो महायशः ।। 7.76.2 ।।
verses.translation: The ascetic declared that he was born in a Shudra family, named Shambuka, and that he sought to attain divinity with his physical body, O greatly renowned Rama.

verses.sanskrit: न मिथ्या ऽहं वदे राम देवलोकजिगीषया । शूद्रं मां विद्धि काकुत्स्थ तप उग्रं समास्थितम् ।। 7.76.3 ।।
verses.translation: Shambuka affirmed to Rama that he was not speaking falsely out of a desire to conquer the world of gods, and asked Rama to know him as a Shudra engaged in severe penance.

verses.sanskrit: भाषतस्तस्य शूद्रस्य खड्गं सुरुचिरप्रभम् । निष्कृष्य कोशाद्विमलं शिरश्चिच्छेद राघवः ।। 7.76.4 ।।
verses.translation: Rama, having drawn his spotless sword of very beautiful luster from its sheath, cut off the head of that Shudra while he was speaking.

verses.sanskrit: तस्मिञ्छूद्रे हते देवाः सेन्द्राः साग्निपुरोगमाः । साधु साध्विति काकुत्स्थं प्रशशंसुर्मुहुर्मुहुः ।। 7.76.5 ।।
verses.translation: When that Shudra was killed, the gods, led by Indra and Agni, repeatedly praised Rama, exclaiming "Excellent! Excellent!"

verses.sanskrit: पुष्पवृष्टिर्महत्यासीद्दिव्यानां सुसुगन्धिनाम् । पुष्पाणां वायुमुक्तानां सर्वतः प्रपपात ह ।। 7.76.6 ।।
verses.translation: A great shower of divine, very fragrant flowers, released by the wind, fell down from all sides.

verses.sanskrit: सुप्रीताश्चाब्रुवन्रामं देवाः सत्यपराक्रमम् । सुरकार्यमिदं सौम्य सुकृतं ते महामते ।। 7.76.7 ।।
verses.translation: The very pleased gods then said to Rama, the one of true valor, "O gentle and great-minded one, this task for the gods has been well accomplished by you."

verses.sanskrit: गृहाण च वरं सौम्य यत्त्वमिच्छस्यरिन्दम । स्वर्गभाङ्नहि शूद्रो ऽयं त्वत्कृते रघुनन्दन ।। 7.76.8 ।।
verses.translation: "O gentle one, subduer of enemies, and delight of the Raghus, accept any boon you desire, for this Shudra will certainly not attain heaven because of your action."

verses.sanskrit: देवानां भाषितं श्रुत्वा राघवः सुसमाहितः । उवाच प्राञ्जलिर्वाक्यं सहस्राक्षं पुरन्दरम् ।। 7.76.9 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words of the gods, Rama, composed and with folded hands, spoke to Indra, the thousand-eyed destroyer of cities.

verses.sanskrit: यदि देवाः प्रसन्ना मे द्विजपुत्रः स जीवतु । दिशन्तु वरमेतं मे इप्सितं परमं मम ।। 7.76.10 ।।
verses.translation: Rama requested that if the gods were pleased with him, they should grant his greatest desire: that the brahmin's son be brought back to life.

verses.sanskrit: ममापचाराद्यातो ऽसौ ब्राह्मणस्यैकपुत्रकः । अप्राप्तकालः कालेन नीतो वैवस्वतक्षयम् ।। 7.76.11 ।।
verses.translation: Rama declared that the brahmin's only son, whose time had not yet come, was taken to Yama's abode due to Rama's own transgression.

verses.sanskrit: तं जीवयथ भद्रं वो नानृतं कर्तुमर्हथ । द्विजस्य संश्रुतो ऽर्थो मे जीवयिष्यामि ते सुतम् ।। 7.76.12 ।।
verses.translation: Rama urged the gods to revive the child, wishing them well, and reminded them that he had promised the brahmin to bring his son back to life, thus he could not allow his word to be false.

verses.sanskrit: राघवस्य तु तद्वाक्यं श्रुत्वा विबुधसत्तमाः । प्रत्युचू राघवं प्रीता देवाः प्रीतिसमन्वितम् ।। 7.76.13 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard Rama's words, the best among the gods, pleased and filled with affection, replied to Rama.

verses.sanskrit: निर्वृतो भव काकुत्स्थ सो ऽस्मिन्नहनि बालकः । जीवितं प्राप्तवान्भूयः समेतश्चापि बन्धुभिः ।। 7.76.14 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, be relieved, for that boy has regained his life and is reunited with his relatives this very day.

verses.sanskrit: यस्मिन्मुहूर्ते काकुत्स्थ शूद्रो ऽयं विनिपातितः । तस्मिन्मुहूर्ते बालो ऽसौ जीवेन समयुज्यत ।। 7.76.15 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, the very moment this Shudra was struck down, that boy was reunited with his life.

verses.sanskrit: स्वस्ति प्राप्नुहि भद्रं ते साधु याम नरर्षभ । अगस्त्यस्याश्रमपदं द्रष्टुमिच्छाम राघव ।। 7.76.16 ।।
verses.translation: May you obtain well-being, O best among men, and may good fortune be yours; we wish to go and see the hermitage of Agastya, O Raghava.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य दीक्षा समाप्ता हि ब्रह्मर्षेः सुमहाद्युतेः । द्वादशं हि गतं वर्षं जलशय्यां समासतः ।। 7.76.17 ।।
verses.translation: The consecratory vow of that brahmin-sage of great splendour is now completed, for the twelfth year has passed as he lay abiding upon a bed of water.

verses.sanskrit: काकुत्स्थ तद्गमिष्यामो मुनिं समभिनन्दितुम् । त्वं चाप्यागच्छ भद्रं ते द्रष्टुं तमृषिसत्तमम् ।। 7.76.18 ।।
verses.translation: O Kakutstha, we shall therefore go to greet that sage, and you too should come, for your welfare, to see that best of sages.

verses.sanskrit: स तथेति प्रतिज्ञाय देवानां रघुनन्दनः । आरुरोह विमानं तं पुष्पकं हेमभूषितम् ।। 7.76.19 ।।
verses.translation: Having thus agreed to the words of the gods, Rama, the delight of the Raghus, ascended that Pushpaka aerial chariot, which was adorned with gold.

verses.sanskrit: ततो देवाः प्रयातास्ते विमानैर्बहुविस्तरैः । रामो ऽप्यनुजगामाशु कुम्भयोनेस्तपोवनम् ।। 7.76.20 ।।
verses.translation: Then, the gods departed in their vast aerial chariots, and Rama also quickly followed them to the hermitage of Agastya.

verses.sanskrit: दृष्ट्वा तु देवान्सम्प्राप्तानगस्त्यस्तपसां निधिः । अर्चयामास धर्मात्मा सर्वांस्तानविशेषतः ।। 7.76.21 ।।
verses.translation: Upon seeing the arrived gods, the righteous Agastya, a treasure-house of austerities, worshipped all of them equally without distinction.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य ततः पूजां सम्पूज्य च महामुनिम् । जग्मुस्ते त्रिदशा हृष्टा नाकपृष्ठं सहानुगैः ।। 7.76.22 ।।
verses.translation: Having accepted the worship and honored the great sage, the delighted gods then departed to heaven with their followers.

verses.sanskrit: गतेषु तेषु काकुत्स्थः पुष्पकादवरुह्य च । ततो ऽभिवादयामास ह्यगस्त्यमृषिसत्तमम् ।। 7.76.23 ।।
verses.translation: After the gods had departed, Rama, the descendant of Kakutstha, descended from the Pushpaka chariot and then saluted Agastya, the foremost of sages.

verses.sanskrit: सो ऽभिवाद्य महात्मानं ज्वलन्तमिव तेजसा । आतिथ्यं परमं प्राप्य निषसाद नराधिपः ।। 7.76.24 ।।
verses.translation: Having saluted the great-souled Agastya, who shone as if with brilliance, and having received excellent hospitality, the king of men, Rama, then sat down.

verses.sanskrit: तमुवाच महातेजाः कुम्भयोनिर्महातपाः । स्वागतं ते नरश्रेष्ठ दिष्ट्या प्राप्तो ऽसि राघव ।। 7.76.25 ।।
verses.translation: The greatly effulgent and great ascetic Agastya said to him, "Welcome to you, O best among men, O scion of Raghu, fortunately you have arrived."

verses.sanskrit: त्वं मे बहुमतो राम गुणैर्बहुभिरुत्तमैः । अतिथिः पूजनीयश्च मम नित्यं हृदि स्थितः ।। 7.76.26 ।।
verses.translation: O Rama, you are highly esteemed by me for your many excellent qualities, and you are a guest worthy of worship, always residing in my heart.

verses.sanskrit: सुरा हि कथयन्ति त्वामागतं शूद्रघातिनम् । ब्राह्मणस्य तु धर्मेण त्वया जीवापितः सुतः ।। 7.76.27 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, the gods speak of you as having come after slaying the Shudra (Shambuka), and by your righteous action, the Brahmin's son was brought back to life.

verses.sanskrit: उष्यतां चेह रजनी सकाशे मम राघव । प्रभाते पुष्पकेण त्वं गन्ता ऽसि पुरमेव हि ।। 7.76.28 ।।
verses.translation: O scion of Raghu, please stay here for the night near me, and in the morning, you will certainly go to your city by the Pushpaka chariot.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं हि नारायणः श्रीमांस्त्वयि सर्वं प्रतिष्ठितम् । त्वं प्रभुः सर्वदेवानां पुरुषस्त्वं सनातनः ।। 7.76.29 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, you are the glorious Narayana, in whom everything is established; you are the lord of all gods, and you are the eternal being.

verses.sanskrit: इदं चाभरणं सौम्य निर्मितं विश्वकर्मणा । दिव्यं दिव्येन वपुषा दीप्यमानं स्वतेजसा । प्रतिगृह्णीष्व काकुत्स्थ मत्प्रियं कुरु राघव ।। 7.76.30 ।।
verses.translation: O gentle Raghava, please accept this divine ornament, created by Vishwakarma, which shines with its own splendor and divine form, and by doing so, fulfill my wish.

verses.sanskrit: दत्तस्य हि पुनर्दाने सुमहत्फलमुच्यते । भरणे हि भवान् शक्तः सेन्द्राणां मरुतामपि ।। 7.76.31 ।।
verses.translation: It is said that giving something again, once it has been given, yields a very great reward, and indeed, you are capable of sustaining even Indra and the Maruts.

verses.sanskrit: त्वं हि शक्तस्तारयितुं सेन्द्रानपि दिवौकसः । तस्मात्प्रदास्ये विधिवत्तत्प्रतीच्छ नराधिप ।। 7.76.32 ।।
verses.translation: Indeed, you are capable of protecting even the gods, including Indra, therefore I will properly give this to you; O king of men, please accept it.

verses.sanskrit: दिव्यमाभरणं चित्रं प्रदीप्तमिव भास्करम् ।। 7.76.33 ।।
verses.translation: This divine and wonderful ornament shines brilliantly like the sun.

verses.sanskrit: अथोवाच महात्मानमिक्ष्वाकूणां महारथः । रामो मतिमतां श्रेष्ठः क्षत्रधर्ममनुस्मरन् ।। 7.76.34 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Rama, the great warrior of the Ikshvakus and the best among the intelligent, spoke to the great-souled one, remembering the duties of a Kshatriya.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिग्रहो ऽयं भगवन्ब्राह्मणस्याविगर्हितः । गृह्णीयां क्षत्रियो ऽहं वै कथं ब्राह्मणपुङ्गव ।। 7.76.35 ।।
verses.translation: Rama questions the sage, asking how he, a Kshatriya, can accept a gift that is considered blameless only for a Brahmana.

verses.sanskrit: ब्राह्मणेन विशेषेण दत्तं तद्वक्तुमर्हसि । एवमुक्तस्तु रामेण प्रत्युवाच महानृषिः ।। 7.76.36 ।।
verses.translation: Rama further requested the sage to explain the reason for accepting such a gift, especially when given by a Brahmana, to which the great sage then replied.

verses.sanskrit: आसन्कृतयुगे राम ब्रह्मभूते पुरायुगे । अपार्थिवाः प्रजाः सर्वाः सुराणां तु शतक्रतुः ।। 7.76.37 ।।
verses.translation: The great sage began by telling Rama that in the ancient Krita Yuga, when the world was pure, all people were without an earthly king, though Indra was the ruler of the gods.

verses.sanskrit: ताः प्रजा देवदेवेशं राजार्थं समुपाद्रवन् । सुराणां स्थापितो राजा त्वया देव शतक्रतुः ।। 7.76.38 ।।
verses.translation: Those people approached the Lord of gods (Brahma) requesting a king, reminding him that he had already established Indra as the king of the gods.

verses.sanskrit: प्रयच्छ नो हि लोकेश पार्थिवं नरपुङ्गवम् । यस्मै पूजां प्रयुञ्जाना धूतपापाश्चरेमहि । न वसामो विना राज्ञा एष नो निश्चयः परः ।। 7.76.39 ।।
verses.translation: The people implored Brahma, the Lord of the worlds, to grant them an earthly king, a best among men, whom they could worship to cleanse their sins and under whose rule they could live, as they declared their supreme resolve not to live without a king.

verses.sanskrit: प्रजानां वचनं श्रुत्वा निश्चयित्वा ऽर्थमुत्तमम् ।। 7.76.40 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard the words of the subjects, and having ascertained their excellent purpose.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ब्रह्मा सुरश्रेष्ठो लोकपालान्सवासवान् । समाहूयाब्रवीत्सर्वांस्तेजोभागान्प्रयच्छत ।। 7.76.41 ।।
verses.translation: Then, Brahma, the best among gods, summoned all the protectors of the worlds, including Indra, and told them to bestow shares of their splendor.

verses.sanskrit: ततो ददुर्लोकपालाः सर्वे भागान्स्वतेजसः । अक्षुपच्च ततो ब्रह्मा यतो जातः क्षुपो नृपः ।। 7.76.42 ।।
verses.translation: Then all the protectors of the worlds gave shares of their own splendor, and then Brahma sneezed, from which King Kṣupa was born.

verses.sanskrit: तं ब्रह्मा लोकपालानां सहांशैः समयोजयत् । ततो ददौ नृपं तासां प्रजानामीश्वरं क्षुपम् ।। 7.76.43 ।।
verses.translation: Brahma then endowed him (Kṣupa) with the shares of splendor from the protectors of the worlds, and then gave King Kṣupa as the ruler of those subjects.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रैन्द्रेण च भागेन महीमाज्ञापयन्नृपः । वारुणेन तु भागेन वपुः पुष्यति पार्थिवः ।। 7.76.44 ।।
verses.translation: There, the king rules the earth with the share of Indra, and with the share of Varuna, the king nourishes his body.

verses.sanskrit: कौबेरेण तु भागेन वित्तमासां ददौ तदा । यस्तु याम्यो ऽभवद्भागस्तेन शास्ति स्म स प्रजाः ।। 7.76.45 ।।
verses.translation: Then, he gave wealth to the people from the northern part (Kubera's domain), and with the southern part (Yama's domain), he ruled his subjects.

verses.sanskrit: तत्रैन्द्रेण नरश्रेष्ठ भागेन रघुनन्दन । प्रतिगृह्णीष्व भद्रं ते तारणार्थं मम प्रभो ।। 7.76.46 ।।
verses.translation: O best among men, O delight of the Raghus, O lord, please accept this eastern part (Indra's domain) for my salvation; may good fortune be yours.

verses.sanskrit: तस्य तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा ऋषेः परमधार्मिकम् । तद्रामः प्रतिजग्राह मुनेराभरणं वरम् ।। 7.76.47 ।।
verses.translation: Having heard that most righteous speech of the sage, Rama accepted that excellent ornament from the sage.

verses.sanskrit: प्रतिगृह्य ततो रामस्तदाभरणमुत्तमम् । आगमं तस्य दीप्तस्य प्रष्टुमेवोपचक्रमे ।। 7.76.48 ।।
verses.translation: Having accepted that excellent ornament, Rama then began to inquire about the origin of that radiant object.

verses.sanskrit: अत्यद्भुतमिदं दिव्यं वपुषा युक्तमुत्तमम् । कथं वा भगवता प्राप्तं कुतो वा केन वा हृतम् ।। 7.76.49 ।।
verses.translation: Rama asked, "How was this extremely wonderful, divine, and excellent form obtained by your revered self, or from where, or by whom was it taken away?"

verses.sanskrit: कुतूहलितया ब्रह्मन्पृच्छामि त्वां महायशः । आश्चर्याणां बहूनां हि निधिः परमको भवान् ।। 7.76.50 ।।
verses.translation: O greatly renowned Brahmana, I ask you out of curiosity, for you are indeed a supreme treasure of many wonders.

verses.sanskrit: एवं ब्रुवति काकुत्स्थे मुनिर्वाक्यमथाब्रवीत् । शृणु राम यथावृत्तं पुरा त्रेतायुगे युगे ।। 7.76.51 ।।
verses.translation: When Rama, the descendant of Kakutstha, spoke thus, the sage then replied, "Listen, O Rama, to what happened formerly in the Treta Yuga."

verses.sanskrit: रमणीयप्रदेशे ऽस्मिन् वने यद्दृष्टवानहम् । आश्चर्यं मे महाबाहो दानमाश्रित्य केवलम् ।। 7.76.52 ।।
verses.translation: O mighty-armed one, I saw a wonder in this beautiful forest region, which was solely concerning the power of charity. इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीये आदिकाव्ये श्रीमदुत्तरकाण्डे षट्सप्ततितमः सर्गः ।। 76 ।। Thus ends the seventy-sixth sarga of Uttara Kanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
